palette: Electric Violet, Matte Obsidian, and Neon Amber (Seoul Midnight)
lighting: Midnight Neon Glow - harsh reflections on wet asphalt, soft bokeh from distant high-rises
instagram_prompts: First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Low-angle micro-scale shot of a wet Hongdae street at midnight. Giant neon signs with Korean characters ('홍대 걷고싶은거리') reflecting in a massive, shimmering puddle on the black asphalt. The world feels impossibly large, every texture of the road magnified. No Lil Mike, no nanobots, no text overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Interior of the RYSE hotel 'Creator' room, Seoul. Extreme close-up on a sleek, dark obsidian-finished desk. A single droplet of condensation from an iced coffee cup sits on the surface, its convex shape magnifying the blurred, glowing Seoul skyline through the floor-to-ceiling glass window in the far background. Impossibly large world. No Lil Mike, no nanobots, no text overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Close-up of the glowing blue '5' and Korean text ('합정역 5번 출구') on a high-tech subway entrance sign at night. The cold, damp metal surface is sharply detailed with tiny scratches and condensation. The background is a soft bokeh of passing car headlights and the frantic energy of a Seoul street. Impossibly large world. No Lil Mike, no nanobots, no text overlays.
